# Archived auto-generated briefs (pre-incident-gate)

These were `account-researcher` / `one-off-writer` outputs for the **original 5 QSOs** picked before the v2 HARD-GATE incident classifier and StandaloneScore mega-IDN cap.

The accounts were either benched (mid-IDN drift) or failed the HARD GATE (no verified on-site violent event):

- **190064 — Our Lady of the Lake (LA)** — std=37.7, FMOL Catholic 11-facility IDN; named-incumbent wrongful-death litigation (Inner Parish Security Corp) → off-thesis for outreach without Legal clearance
- **310009 — Clara Maass (NJ)** — failed HARD GATE (no verified on-site event); kept for reference
- **330101 — NewYork-Presbyterian (NY)** — std=46, 8-facility mid-IDN; fits the "no mega-IDN" rule we corrected against
- **450046 — CHRISTUS Spohn (TX)** — failed HARD GATE (Nov 2024 was a hoax; May 2022 was transported-to)

The v2 portfolio of 5 QSOs is documented in `documents/qso_briefs.md` with hand-written briefs at `documents/qso-briefs/qso-{1..5}-*.md`. Auto-generated briefs are no longer the source of truth; the hand-written ones are.

Companion reference: `documents/qso-briefs/bench/` holds the human-edited bench briefs for these same accounts with the failure rationale spelled out.
